compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
G4 child-consistency review (parent 3fe1 + this reconciler-sweep child, whole sibling roster considered). Two high-confidence cross-child findings; the broad-except / characterization-test / rollback scope is otherwise clean and consistent with the parent.

---
FINDING 1 — Dependency gap + conflicting assumptions: migrated reconciler diagnostics log to a HANDLER-LESS logger in a NAMESPACE the substrate never wires up.

criteria: [parent SC2 "failures that used to vanish silently are now observable on a stderr logger"; parent AC1 logging substrate; this child AC: "Migrate stderr-diagnostic prints (reconcile.py, __main__.py, etc.) to the named logger"]
location: src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/__main__.py (main, ~L266), src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/reconcile.py; vs sibling 8fbd (logging substrate) file_impact {src/rebar/__init__.py, cli.py, _cli/__init__.py, mcp_server.py}
evidence:
  - The reconciler is a SEPARATE SUBPROCESS entrypoint: `python -m rebar_reconciler` with its own main() in __main__.py:266, launched by the GHA reconcile-bridge workflow / inject-and-heal.sh / e2e probes (scripts/jira-pressure-test/*.sh:75,135 `python -m rebar_reconciler "$@" 2>&1`). README.md:215-225 + src/rebar/_engine.py:75-96 confirm it ships as on-disk package DATA and runs out-of-process.
  - Sibling 8fbd installs a stderr StreamHandler ONLY at the CLI main (cli.py / _cli/__init__.py) and the MCP main (mcp_server.py:main), and the NullHandler on the "rebar" ROOT logger in src/rebar/__init__.py. It explicitly does NOT touch the reconciler's __main__.main(). Confirmed: no StreamHandler/basicConfig exists anywhere under src/rebar today (grep: 0 hits) — matches the audit's "no handler configured anywhere".
  - The reconciler's modules import as the TOP-LEVEL `rebar_reconciler` package (from rebar_reconciler import acli, etc.; _engine.py:82-85 prepends the engine dir to PYTHONPATH for this), so the convention's `logging.getLogger(__name__)` resolves to `rebar_reconciler.<module>` — NOT under the `rebar` root logger where 8fbd attaches the NullHandler/handler. The reconciler already does this in ~16 modules (apply_outbound.py:27, batch_dispatch.py:27, acli_subprocess.py:23, ...).
  - rebar_reconciler/__init__.py is empty (one comment line) — no NullHandler, no handler. __main__.main() installs no handler.
scenarios:
  - After this child migrates a `print(..., file=sys.stderr)` INFO/diagnostic in reconcile.py/__main__.py to `logger.info(...)`/`logger.warning(...)`, the reconciler subprocess has NO handler on the `rebar_reconciler` logger tree → INFO is dropped entirely; WARNING+ only reaches Python's last-resort stderr in a different format. A diagnostic that USED to be visible (and captured by the probes' `2>&1` side-car) now vanishes — the exact inversion of parent SC2.
impact: This pairing cannot meet its own AC ("migrate prints to the named logger") AND parent SC2 ("observable on a stderr logger") simultaneously, because no sibling stands up a handler for the reconciler subprocess / the `rebar_reconciler` logger namespace. The substrate child's two-entrypoint design (CLI + MCP) silently omits rebar's THIRD entrypoint. Absence cross-checked against the whole roster: no sibling (8fbd, 16a1, 8d01, d360, ceaa, fd72, 0ec1) installs a handler at __main__.main() or bridges rebar_reconciler.* to the rebar root logger.
checklist_item: dependency gap; conflicting assumptions
suggested_fix: Either (a) add to this child's scope (or re-scope 8fbd) a stderr StreamHandler install at rebar_reconciler/__main__.py:main() — the reconciler's own entrypoint — and/or (b) explicitly bridge the `rebar_reconciler` logger to the `rebar` root (propagation won't help across distinct root names) so migrated diagnostics actually reach stderr. Add an acceptance bullet here asserting reconciler-subprocess diagnostics land on stderr, and add a cross-dep on 8fbd. If the decision is to NOT migrate reconciler prints (keep file=sys.stderr) given it is a distinct entrypoint, strike the "migrate stderr-diagnostic prints ... to the named logger" bullet to remove the contradiction.

---
FINDING 2 — Consumer impact: the `ERROR:`-prefix stderr contract that operators/probes grep for is broken by the print->logger migration.

criteria: [parent SC2 stdout/stderr observability; this child AC: "keep any acli/jira data output semantics intact"]
location: src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/__main__.py:401-417 (and the `ERROR:`-prefixed stderr prints), e2e probes scripts/jira-pressure-test/e2e_validation_probe.sh / e2e_field_validation_probe.sh
evidence:
  - __main__.py:401-404 documents that diagnostics are emitted to stderr specifically so "probes that look for the `ERROR:` prefix" and "the probe's unfiltered side-car log" capture them; __main__.py:413-416 prints `ERROR: --filter-local-ids ...` to stderr.
  - The probes capture reconciler output via `python -m rebar_reconciler "$@" 2>&1` (e2e_validation_probe.sh:75, e2e_field_validation_probe.sh:135) and grep that combined stream.
scenarios:
  - Migrating these `print("ERROR: ...", file=sys.stderr)` lines to `logger.error(...)` changes the line prefix/format (logger default has no `ERROR:` token unless a formatter is configured — and per Finding 1 no handler/formatter is configured for this subprocess at all), so a probe greppping for `ERROR:` stops matching.
impact: A consumer (the jira-pressure-test probes) silently loses its failure signal — a residual-reference/consumer-impact mode unguarded by any sibling. This is exactly the kind of "best-effort migration that breaks a machine contract" the parent's stdout-is-sacred section guards for, but on the stderr/probe side.
checklist_item: consumer impact; residual references
suggested_fix: When migrating reconciler diagnostics, either preserve the `ERROR:` token via a configured formatter on the reconciler handler (depends on Finding 1's handler existing), or explicitly carve out the probe-contract `ERROR:`-prefixed stderr prints as data-like output that stays as `print(..., file=sys.stderr)` (parallel to the CLI "stdout data prints stay" carve-out). Add an AC bullet asserting the probe `ERROR:`-grep contract is preserved.

---
Modes checked and clean for this pairing: implicit shared state (the _errors.py helpers are read-only/shared-by-design, characterization-tested first — fine); scope overlap (reconciler package is uniquely owned by this child; no other sweep touches _engine/rebar_reconciler/); ordering violation (LATE/last sequencing + blocks 0ec1 + downstream of 16a1 is consistent). The two findings above are the dependency-gap/conflicting-assumptions and consumer-impact modes.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
G4 re-confirmation (parent 3fe1 + this reconciler-sweep child, whole roster considered). Independently re-verified the two prior cross-child findings against source; both hold high-confidence.

Verified facts:
- `rebar_reconciler` is a TOP-LEVEL package put on PYTHONPATH for subprocess use only (_engine.py:75-96, `python -m rebar_reconciler`), so its ~16 `logging.getLogger(__name__)` loggers resolve under `rebar_reconciler.*`, NOT the `rebar` root.
- `rebar_reconciler/__init__.py` is a single comment line — no NullHandler.
- No `StreamHandler` exists anywhere under `src/rebar` today (0 hits) — matches the audit's "no handler configured anywhere"; substrate child 8fbd only installs handlers at CLI + MCP mains and a NullHandler on the `rebar` ROOT, leaving the reconciler subprocess + `rebar_reconciler.*` namespace unwired.
- Probes capture `python -m rebar_reconciler "$@" 2>&1` (e2e_validation_probe.sh:75, e2e_field_validation_probe.sh:135) and grep `^(FILTERED|filter:|OK:|ERROR:)` (17 sites), consuming the `ERROR:`-prefixed stderr prints (__main__.py:413-416, documented at :398-404).

FINDING 1 — Dependency gap + conflicting assumptions: this child's AC "migrate stderr-diagnostic prints (reconcile.py, __main__.py) to the named logger" cannot coexist with parent SC2 "observable on a stderr logger", because no sibling stands up a handler for the reconciler's third subprocess entrypoint or bridges `rebar_reconciler.*` to the `rebar` root. Migrated INFO is dropped; WARNING+ falls to Python's last-resort handler in a different format. Cross-checked whole roster: no sibling installs a handler at `rebar_reconciler/__main__.main()`.
  suggested_fix: re-scope 8fbd (or this child) to install a stderr StreamHandler at `rebar_reconciler/__main__.py:main()` and/or bridge `rebar_reconciler` to `rebar`; add an AC asserting reconciler-subprocess diagnostics land on stderr; add cross-dep on 8fbd. Else strike the print->logger migration bullet to remove the contradiction.

FINDING 2 — Consumer impact / residual references: migrating the `ERROR:`-prefixed stderr prints to `logger.error(...)` breaks the `^ERROR:` grep contract the jira-pressure-test probes depend on (no formatter emits an `ERROR:` token, and per Finding 1 no handler/formatter is configured for this subprocess). A consumer silently loses its failure signal — the stderr-side analogue of the parent's stdout-is-sacred constraint.
  suggested_fix: preserve the `ERROR:` token via a configured formatter on the reconciler handler, OR carve out the probe-contract `ERROR:`-prefixed stderr prints as data-like output that stays `print(..., file=sys.stderr)` (parallel to the CLI stdout-data carve-out). Add an AC asserting the probe `ERROR:`-grep contract is preserved.

Modes clean for this pairing: implicit shared state (_errors.py helpers shared read-only, characterization-tested first); scope overlap (reconciler package uniquely owned — no other sweep touches _engine/rebar_reconciler/); ordering violation (LATE/last + blocks 0ec1 + downstream of 16a1 is consistent).

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
PASS 1 review — criterion E4 (assumption/premise verification). I probed every codebase assertion in the plan via Read/Grep rather than relying on cached knowledge.

AFFIRMATIONS (assertions verified true against source):
- "`apply_outbound.py:416,441`" — VERIFIED: `is_not_found(exc)` at apply_outbound.py:416 and `getattr(exc, "status_code", None) in (404, 410, 403)` at :441, both inside `except JiraAPIError as exc:` blocks.
- "`batch_dispatch.py:77,307`" — VERIFIED: `exc.status_code` 4xx fast-fail at batch_dispatch.py:77 (in `except JiraAPIError`), and `http_status(exc)` at :307 (`_is_illegal_transition_400`).
- "the `_engine/rebar_reconciler/_errors.py` helpers already exist" — VERIFIED: `_errors.py:6 http_status(...)` and `:22 is_not_found(...)` are present and implemented.
- "`reconcile.py:1155` `except BaseException ... raise`" — VERIFIED verbatim: reconcile.py:1155 `except BaseException as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — must re-raise after recording` then `raise` at :1157.
- "Migrate stderr-diagnostic prints (`reconcile.py`, `__main__.py`, etc.)" — VERIFIED such prints exist (reconcile.py has ~20 print sites; __main__.py has stderr `ERROR:`/`OK:` prints), and __main__.py also emits stdout data output (report at :162, json result at :254), corroborating the "keep acli/jira data output semantics intact" caveat.

FINDINGS:

- [ ] [E4] AC4 asserts "This package removed from the allowlist", but the ONLY current allowlist entry for this package is a `T201` per-file-ignore that pyproject.toml documents as a PERMANENT legitimate-print surface, not a removable transitional entry — verify which allowlist AC4 means and correct the wording.
  criteria: [E4]
  location: Acceptance Criteria line 4 ("This package removed from the allowlist") vs pyproject.toml:202-232
  evidence:
    - pyproject.toml:226-231 (the only per-file-ignore touching this package's BLE001 history) reads: "reconciler subprocess — BLE001 SWEPT by 0569 ... KEEPS T201: the reconciler is a subprocess whose stderr ... + stdout ... prints ARE its operational output — a legitimate-print surface."
    - pyproject.toml:232 `"src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/*" = ["T201"]` — a KEPT T201 ignore, with no BLE001 entry present for this package. There is no remaining BLE001 allowlist line to remove; the T201 line is explicitly NOT slated for removal.
    - The plan body's own caveats ("Migrate stderr-diagnostic prints ... keep any acli/jira data output semantics intact"; rollback/cleanup swallows "stay intentionally broad") are consistent with T201 staying — so "removed from the allowlist" is ambiguous/likely inaccurate as written.
  suggested_fix: Reword AC4 to name the specific removal precisely — e.g. "the BLE001 ignore for this package is removed (no BLE001 per-file-ignore line for src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/ remains); the T201 entry at pyproject.toml:232 stays as a documented legitimate-print surface" — so the criterion is verifiable.

- [ ] [E4] The "add characterization tests FIRST" premise treats the safety net as not-yet-existing, but `tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/test_errors_classification.py` already pins `is_not_found`/`http_status` and is explicitly tagged to this ticket — clarify whether the AC is "author new" or "confirm/extend existing".
  criteria: [E4]
  location: "What" bullet 1 + AC1 ("Characterization tests cover ... before remediation") vs tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/test_errors_classification.py:1-15
  evidence:
    - test_errors_classification.py:1-8 docstring: "Characterization tests for the reconciler's HTTP error-classification helpers. Pins the CURRENT behavior of `http_status` / `is_not_found` BEFORE the error-handling sweep (epic ring-gun-jot, ticket 0569) touches the broad `except` sites..." — i.e. this file is named as 0569's own safety net.
    - test_errors_classification.py:15 `from rebar_reconciler._errors import http_status, is_not_found` and helper exception classes (`_StatusCodeExc`, `_CodeExc`, `_BothExc`) exercise both `.status_code` and `.code` paths — the classification is already characterized.
  suggested_fix: State in the plan whether AC1 is satisfied by extending the existing test_errors_classification.py (and to what coverage delta) or by adding new tests, so a reviewer can verify the "FIRST" ordering claim against an already-present file rather than assuming it is absent.

Note: both findings are clarity/verifiability of stated premises, not blockers — the underlying remediation scope is well-grounded and the file/line references all check out. (Separate from the G4 logging-substrate findings already on this ticket, which I did not re-litigate.)

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: Reconciler sweep landed (commit 2341d628). Plan-review PASS (0 blocking, 1 advisory, 4 indeterminate), signed serially at the commit. Characterization tests for is_not_found/http_status added FIRST (6 tests, pin status_code-over-code precedence, non-int->None, only-404). All 20 broad-excepts to floor with justified noqa, ZERO behavior change: 8 cleanup-and-reraise (incl. 3 BaseException kept since they re-raise), 4 rollback-must-not-mask-original, 8 fail-open. Prints kept (operational subprocess output, T201 retained, BLE001 removed). Code-review subagent (general-purpose recommended going forward; this used Explore): clean — verified BaseException re-raises, rollback-no-mask, behavior preservation. Reconciler unit suite 977 pass; full suite 3215 passed/13 skipped run ALONE (serialized), HEAD stable. make lint green.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
Completion verification: **PASS** — all four acceptance criteria are met.

**AC1 — Characterization tests cover `is_not_found`/`http_status` classification before remediation.**
Verified: `tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/test_errors_classification.py` contains 6 tests (lines 41–74) pinning `http_status` (status_code vs code precedence, non-int→None, urllib style) and `is_not_found` (true only for 404, false for 410/403/500/non-HTTP). The file header explicitly states it is the safety net authored before the 0569 sweep touches the broad-except sites. ✅

**AC2 — Broad-excepts meet the observability floor; body-inspecting ones kept-broad-and-logged or narrowed with a passing test.**
Verified: All broad-excepts carry `# noqa: BLE001` with inline justifications. Body-inspecting catches (e.g., `apply_outbound.py:261,290,333` and `batch_dispatch.py:268,285,400`) pair the broad catch with `logger.warning(...)` calls recording the failure. The `is_not_found`/`http_status` sites (`apply_outbound.py:416,441`, `batch_dispatch.py:307`) remain narrow at `JiraAPIError` — unchanged from before and covered by the characterization tests. ✅

**AC3 — Rollback/cleanup swallows remain broad with a one-line justification; none silently masks an error.**
Verified: `reconcile.py:1155` — `except BaseException as exc: # noqa: BLE001 — must re-raise after recording` then `raise` at :1157. `batch_dispatch.py:200,203` — rollback handler with "never masked" justification, `write_err` re-raised at :241. `batch_dispatch.py:239` — alert-write "must not mask original error". `apply_planning.py:249`, `binding_store.py:170,212` — atomic-write cleanup with re-raise. ✅

**AC4 — Package removed from the allowlist; `make lint` + full test suite pass.**
Verified: `pyproject.toml:226–232` shows the reconciler comment block reads "BLE001 SWEPT by 0569" and the remaining entry is `"src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/*" = ["T201"]` — BLE001 is absent. No other per-file-ignore entry for this package exists. Inline `# noqa: BLE001` on ~60 sites carry the justifications. The last commit comment reports make lint green and full suite 3215 passed/13 skipped. ✅

**Additional note (informational, not a blocker):** The G4 reviews raised concerns about logging-handler wiring for the reconciler subprocess. The implementation addresses this: `__main__.py:280–282` calls `install_stderr_handler("rebar_reconciler")` at entry, and `__init__.py:12` attaches a NullHandler on the `rebar_reconciler` root. The prints that carry the `ERROR:`-prefix probe contract remain as `print(..., file=sys.stderr)` (not migrated to logger calls), so the probe grep contract is intact.

compiled_state.description:
# Sweep: reconciler (_engine/rebar_reconciler/) — LATE, test-heavy (HTTP-classification sensitive)

The highest-behavior-change-risk batch — do it LAST. Many broad-excepts feed exception-type-dependent
bodies (`is_not_found(exc)`, `http_status(exc)`, `getattr(exc, "status_code", ...)` at
`apply_outbound.py:416,441`, `batch_dispatch.py:77,307`). Narrowing these changes which exceptions reach
the body and thus HTTP 404/error classification — so add characterization tests FIRST, then remediate.

## What
- Add characterization tests around `is_not_found`/`http_status` error classification before touching the
  catches (the `_engine/rebar_reconciler/_errors.py` helpers already exist).
- Bring broad-excepts in `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/` to the observability floor. Body-inspecting
  catches stay broad-but-logged unless a narrow is proven safe by a test.
- Rollback/cleanup swallows (e.g. `batch_dispatch.py` "rollback must not mask original error";
  `reconcile.py:1155` `except BaseException ... raise`) stay intentionally broad with a justification —
  do not narrow.
- Migrate stderr-diagnostic prints (`reconcile.py`, `__main__.py`, etc.) to the named logger; keep any
  acli/jira data output semantics intact.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Characterization tests cover `is_not_found`/`http_status` classification before remediation.
- [ ] Broad-excepts in `_engine/rebar_reconciler/` meet the observability floor; body-inspecting ones are
      kept-broad-and-logged or narrowed only with a passing test.
- [ ] Rollback/cleanup swallows remain broad with a one-line justification; none silently masks an error.
- [ ] This package removed from the allowlist; `make lint` + full test suite pass.

file_impact.reason: characterization tests pin is_not_found/http_status before remediation
20 broad-excepts to floor (cleanup-reraise, rollback-no-mask, fail-open); BaseException kept where re-raised; prints kept (operational)
reconciler drops BLE001, keeps T201 (operational subprocess output)

compiled_state.title: Sweep: reconciler (_engine/rebar_reconciler) — LATE, test-heavy (HTTP-classification sensitive)
